name: kyo
description: 興 — games that invent themselves, and judge their own fun. From a seed, a ruleset for an abstract board game is born (board size, how you place, how you win). A generic engine plays any ruleset; a generic alpha-beta AI plays it hundreds of times against itself and against random, and measures whether the game is any good: does skill matter (can a thinking player beat a random one?), does it resolve, is it fair, is it the right length? Dull rules are discarded; promising ones are given a name and a rulebook. A game designer with no designer. Deterministic; the core knows neither DOM nor network.
